title: Boarding the Express
narrative_context: You board the Paris night express at 22:00. The platform is crowded, but you find your seat in the first-class compartment. A mysterious elderly woman sits across from you.
character_opening_dialogue: Bonsoir. This seat — it remains empty, yes? How fortunate. These trains get more crowded every year.
prompt: Respond to Madame Dubois. Introduce yourself and ask about the journey to Paris.
character_advance_dialogue: Ah, first time to Paris? How delightful. You picked an interesting night to travel — there are some... unusual passengers aboard tonight.
character_pause_dialogue: Pardon? I didn't quite catch that. Could you repeat more clearly?

title: The Theft Discovery
narrative_context: Three hours into the journey, an alarm sounds. The train conductor bursts into your compartment looking panicked. 'A valuable diamond necklace has been stolen from the private train car!'
character_opening_dialogue: Excuse me! Has anyone seen anything suspicious? A priceless necklace vanished from the lockbox. The police will meet us at the next station in two hours.
prompt: Ask the conductor questions about the theft. Who had access? When was it discovered?
character_advance_dialogue: Smart questions. Only three people had access to that car today: myself, an aristocrat, and a young musician. Nobody has left their compartment.
character_pause_dialogue: I'm sorry, I don't understand what you're asking. The situation is urgent — please focus.

title: Interviewing the Guard
narrative_context: You volunteer to help investigate. The conductor takes you to meet the security guard, Alex, a stern man in his fifties who was protecting the car when the theft occurred.
character_opening_dialogue: I don't need help from amateurs. But if you want to waste time... I was on duty the entire period. Nobody suspicious passed by my post.
prompt: Convince Alex to tell you more about the people he saw. Was anyone acting nervous?
character_advance_dialogue: Fine. Yes — the aristocrat, Comte de Rêveurs, seemed unusually agitated. He kept checking his watch. But that proves nothing.
character_pause_dialogue: Look, I answered already. Don't test my patience.

title: The Musician's Secret
narrative_context: In the dining car, you encounter Sophie, a young classical musician who was near the private car. She seems nervous, avoiding eye contact.
character_opening_dialogue: Oh... hello. I heard about the necklace. Terrible business. I hope they find it soon. I really need to practice my violin...
prompt: Try to put Sophie at ease. Ask her about her journey and what she knows about the theft.
character_advance_dialogue: You seem genuine. Okay... I was near the car earlier, but I wasn't in it. I heard voices — angry French. Someone said, 'It has to disappear tonight.' But I didn't see who.
character_pause_dialogue: I-I don't want any trouble. Please don't ask me questions.

title: The Comte's Confession
narrative_context: You find the Comte in the bar car, drinking heavily. He's clearly agitated about something beyond the theft.
character_opening_dialogue: Ah, another detective? Everyone on this train wants to play inspector today. The necklace was insured. I have no reason to steal from myself.
prompt: Challenge the Comte's logic. If it was insured, why does he seem so nervous?
character_advance_dialogue: Because... *looks around nervously* ...that necklace wasn't mine to insure. It was entrusted to me for a very important person. If it's missing, there will be consequences far worse than money.
character_pause_dialogue: You're getting too close to things you shouldn't know about.

title: Madame's True Identity
narrative_context: You return to your compartment and find Madame Dubois packing her belongings. She looks up calmly when you enter, revealing something shocking.
character_opening_dialogue: Sit down. I need to explain everything. I am not who you think I am. I am Inspecteur Dubois, retired. I've been tracking this necklace for months.
prompt: Ask Madame Dubois about her investigation and what the necklace really is.
character_advance_dialogue: It's not just a necklace — it contains microfilm with classified information. The Comte was supposed to deliver it safely, but someone on this train wants to intercept it.
character_pause_dialogue: Are you working with them? Because if you are, this conversation ends now.

title: The Real Thief
narrative_context: Working together, you and Madame Dubois set a trap. When the thief attempts to retrieve the necklace from its new hiding place, Alex intervenes. The true criminal is revealed.
character_opening_dialogue: I'm sorry, but I've worked too long for this moment. The microfilm is worth more than you could imagine. I had to do this.
prompt: Respond to the revelation. How do you feel about Alex's betrayal? What do you say?
character_advance_dialogue: I appreciate your understanding. I needed someone smart enough to help expose this corruption. Will you help me take this to the right authorities?
character_pause_dialogue: You don't understand the world you're in. This doesn't end here.

title: Arrival in Paris
narrative_context: The train pulls into Gare de Lyon. The morning sun hits the Paris skyline. Police are waiting on the platform. The adventure ends, but many questions remain.
character_opening_dialogue: Thank you. Paris is safe because of your courage. This is where we part. Remember — sometimes the most important mysteries have no simple answers.
prompt: Say your final goodbye to Madame Dubois. Reflect on what you've learned.
character_advance_dialogue: I believe you will do great things. Here — take my card. If you ever need help, call me.
character_pause_dialogue: I hope you learned something today.
